title: Differences between Rebuild and the Series
display_title: Differences between Rebuild and the Series
wikitext:
[]: just trying to be funny folks, don't read too much into it             Quote Post by Dream Focus » Thu Oct 16, 2008 8:04 pm I saw the first part and stopped watching it after noticing some horrible changes. Misato doesn't tease Shinji when she meets him saying, "I thought you'd be cuter". Misato doesn't steal batteries from an army vehicle for her car, with Shinji asking "should we be taking those?" She doesn't whine about her dress and how many payments she had left on her car. Misato doesn't get lost in NERV trying to find her way around. How much else is changed in this? Don't take out all the funny bits that give the characters emotion! How many minutes did they cut out, and how many seconds did they add in? And didn't they already redo some animation in a previous re-release of the series? Millennium version or something? --- Originally the station that aired it, argued there was too much violence in it, and convinced Sega, its main sponsor, to cut funding. I wonder if they'll add in additional violence now? Also Asuka should have larger breast, wear less clothing, carry a miniature flame thrower and have a pet mechanical metal hawk that rides on her shoulder. Oh, and if instead of an EVA, she could ride on a flying unicorn, or a sleek fire breathing dragon, that'd be totally cool! In fact, give Asuka the dragon, and let her tease Shinji for ending up on a unicorn.

[]: Re: just trying to be funny folks, don't read too much into             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Oct 16, 2008 8:17 pm Dream Focus wrote: I saw the first part and stopped watching it after noticing some horrible changes. [list] [*]Misato doesn't tease Shinji when she meets him saying, "I thought you'd be cuter". [*]Misato doesn't steal batteries from an army vehicle for her car, with Shinji asking "should we be taking those?" [*]She doesn't whine about her dress and how many payments she had left on her car. [*]Misato doesn't get lost in NERV trying to find her way around.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream Focus » Thu Oct 16, 2008 8:56 pm File Name: EvanGellion 1.01 You are not alone.MP4 4 Fiile size: 425,290KB File Type: MPEG-4 Movie Duration: 1 hour and 38 minutes and 17 seconds. So, how long is what everyone else has, and how big is the file size? Post any decent links you have please. Severe delays in official releases, will turn even the most honest of men into a thief, but only for a duration.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream Focus » Thu Oct 16, 2008 9:15 pm http://anime.mikomi.org/series/1437.html I just looked it up. Others noted it missing also. That guy listed all the changes rather well. * The scenes are cut in a way to make the whole story more serious - most of the funny dialogs featuring Misato have been shortened or even eliminated, Shinji doesn't gasp for air when he's drowning in LCL, fewer Kensuke scenes, fewer Pen-Pen scenes etc. so there's hardly any comedy remaining (while the episode 2 fanservice is left intact).

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Oct 16, 2008 10:00 pm Dream Focus wrote: So, how long is what everyone else has, and how big is the file size? Post any decent links you have please. Severe delays in official releases, will turn even the most honest of men into a thief, but only for a duration.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Oct 16, 2008 10:22 pm I'm not sure whether to laugh right now or go grumble in annoyance.

[]:             Quote Post by Dramamine » Thu Oct 16, 2008 10:49 pm ...Rebuild of Evangelion isn't a remake, it's a retelling, (or something completely different) wait until the second movie is released before you make your judgments on 'plot retarding' I'm sure the next installment of RoE will blow our minds.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Oct 17, 2008 12:26 pm You stopped watching after 10 minutes? Wow that seems fair and reasonable. You're in the perfect position to judge it. At least watch the thing before you start bitching.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream Focus » Fri Oct 17, 2008 12:39 pm Why would I watch through something if I remember perfectly what happens? Reruns are for people with bad memories. I notice the parts that I liked in the original now missing. I'd watch the fight scenes again if they were that much different, but don't see any reason to watch the rest of it. The version I downloaded doesn't look that great in full screen mode, it just playing in a rather small window on my screen. Anyone got a link to a version that is of great quality for full screen mode? I don't suppose anyone has a version with just the new fight scenes or whatever else was actually added, do they?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Oct 17, 2008 1:53 pm ( V gapes) ...? The entire point behind Rebuild, is they are CHANGING things, but didn't want to scare fans off, so they said "ok, the REALLY divergent stuff happens in movie 2; movie 1 will be more or less the same....in order to win back those fans who are so obsessive they care if we take out a line or two of dialogue from the original" by the end, the final half hour is very different indeed you see the most common criticism was that it didn't change ENOUGH, but I thuoght it had the "light touch" needed not to scare off fans (plus, they ARE going to change things) We have been developing quite the list of "Things that Rebuild changed" and little things like "they took out the battery joke" are relatively minor because we know they have to condense things for time. You seriously "didn't bother" to watch until the end?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Oct 17, 2008 2:00 pm You'll have to go to toshokan or something, providing download links or torrent links in the forum is prohibited. Or you can try PMing someone who looks like they know where to get it.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Oct 17, 2008 2:57 pm I'd like to point something out. This kid has 11 posts so he's clearly just signed up to this website. And he's bitching about wasting his time watching 10 minutes of a Retelling of Evangelion. And yet...he sees these 10 minutes, is disappointed, and then finds an Evangelion website online, creates a profile and then responds to every post made in reaction to his. What wastes more time? Watching a 90 minute movie and just enjoying yourself and going through all that BS just to bitch about it online? When did this page become IMDB?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Oct 17, 2008 3:07 pm Someone lock this. If the guy wants to find out the differences in the film he should watch it. END OF. It's not like it's hard to torrent...

[]:             Quote Post by Dream Focus » Fri Oct 17, 2008 6:27 pm Rather defensive aren't we? I wasn't aware there was any major changes. From what I read, they just redid the animation quality scene by scene, and added a minute or two extra for the battles. If it is that different, I'll go have a look then. And I wasn't aware download links were forbidden here, or I wouldn't ask for them. Sorry about that. And yet...he sees these 10 minutes, is disappointed, and then finds an Evangelion website online, creates a profile and then responds to every post made in reaction to his.

[]:             Quote Post by sedemihcrA » Fri Oct 17, 2008 6:35 pm Dream Focus wrote: I wasn't aware there was any major changes. From what I read, they just redid the animation quality scene by scene, and added a minute or two extra for the battles. If it is that different, I'll go have a look then.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Fri Oct 17, 2008 6:49 pm And I wasn't aware download links were forbidden here, or I wouldn't ask for them. Sorry about that.

[]:             Quote Post by Rommel » Fri Oct 17, 2008 7:55 pm V wrote: The entire point behind Rebuild, is they are CHANGING things, but didn't want to scare fans off, so they said "ok, the REALLY divergent stuff happens in movie 2; movie 1 will be more or less the same....in order to win back those fans who are so obsessive they care if we take out a line or two of dialogue from the original"

[]: empirical judgements? How dare you judge the empire!             Quote Post by Dream Focus » Fri Oct 17, 2008 10:28 pm Are just silly. If you haven't watched it yet, why would you make empirical judgments about it? Just because some dude tells you it's one way, doesn't mean you should believe him.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Fri Oct 17, 2008 10:34 pm As for the illegal link thing, I thought Japanese copyright law said-

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 18, 2008 2:39 am 1) Play the ball, not the man, guys 2) Pretty much every nation has signed up to the Berne Convention on copyright, even idiosyncratic places like the US -- so fansubs are technically naughty regardless of licensing status. Different companies take different attitudes, from having DMCA notices sent out to regarding them as helpful word-of-mouth. @Dream Focus If you want to find fansubs, you are better off going to more generally anime focussed places for discussion like animesuki or 4chan's /a/, and wait until someone takes pity on the n00b. I am aware of 3 different fansubs for Rebuild 1.01, 4 if you count different encodes; sizes from 700 to 1400MB; I watched the smallest of these, the conveniently CD-sized one. As they are covering the ground of 6 TV episodes in about the same running time as 4 of them, then obviously material is going to be cut -- especially when they want to show off in the big fight scene at the climax. I'm more worried about the new material -- including a preview of the next movie that is 80% entirely new material inconsistent with the series and more akin to bad fan-fics.

1.01, 4 if you count different encodes; sizes from 700 to 1400MB; I watched the smallest of these, the conveniently CD-sized one. As they are covering the ground of 6 TV episodes in about the same running time as 4 of them, then obviously material is going to be cut -- especially when they want to show off in the big fight scene at the climax. I'm more worried about the new material -- including a preview of the next movie that is 80% entirely new material inconsistent with the series and more akin to bad fan-fics.
